name: Mexican Chocolate Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat the eggs at medium speed on an electric mixer until frothy.
2. Gradually add sugar, beating until thick.
3. Heat half and half in a 3-quart saucepan over low heat until hot.
4. Gradually stir about one-fourth of the hot mixture into the eggs; add the remaining hot mixture, stirring constantly.
5. Cook over low heat until the mixture is slightly thickened and reaches 165 degrees Fahrenheit.
6. Remove from heat and stir in chocolate syrup and the remaining ingredients.
7. Cool in the refrigerator.
8. Pour into a freezer can on a 1-gallon freezer.
9. Freeze according to manufacturer's instructions.
10. Let ripen at least 1 hour.
Yield: about 1 gallon
